purpose: Filter out technicians that cannot legally or operationally take the job before expensive ranking.
required_feature_groups: credentials, geography, language, customer restrictions, schedule, tool eligibility
training_cadence: rule deploy as needed; classifier weekly
ui_surfaces: none directly; powers candidate set and watchouts
notes: Never treat credentials as proof of observed skill.

purpose: Build overall trust score and decomposition shown on the card.
required_feature_groups: completeness, consistency, support evidence, calibration, in-distribution, neighbor agreement
label_or_target: stable trust score calibrated against realized outcomes
notes: Human-readable metric; do not derive directly from embeddings.

purpose: Compute offer acceptance / follow-through behavior for operations.
required_feature_groups: offer count, acceptance count, decline count, cancel count, response lag
notes: Direct operational metric; also feeds universal state encoder.

purpose: Compute transparent lifetime and rolling job completion counts.
required_feature_groups: completed jobs, cancelled jobs, redispatch jobs
notes: Display metric and evidence depth baseline.

purpose: Compute customer/PM rating summaries with evidence counts.
required_feature_groups: rating mean, rating count, complaint count, compliment count
notes: Shrink small sample sizes aggressively.

purpose: Predict likelihood of last-minute cancellation or no-show on a proposed assignment.
required_feature_groups: acceptance history, lateness history, travel load, schedule conflicts, customer/site friction, freshness
notes: Strong early value model; easy first production win.

purpose: Predict risk that ETA or arrival SLA will be missed.
required_feature_groups: drive time variance, local/regional radius, traffic/time windows, access friction, punctuality history
notes: Use site/time buckets to improve calibration.

purpose: Predict check-in/access delay risk for specific site contexts.
required_feature_groups: access requirements, contact responsiveness, site friction history, after-hours rules, permits
notes: Use categorical explainers for watchouts.

purpose: Predict first-pass success probability and QC defect risk.
required_feature_groups: step performance, rework loops, validation logs, similar-job history, domain depth
ui_surfaces: Fit & Trust, Skills & Certs, Risk & Conditions
notes: Primary quality predictor for dispatch and scoring.

purpose: Predict whether photo/docs/serial/label requirements will fail or be incomplete.
required_feature_groups: artifact completeness, photo quality, note quality, signoff history, closeout admin discipline
ui_surfaces: Fit & Trust, Risk & Conditions, Past Jobs
notes: Great for trust decomposition and closeout quality.

purpose: Predict probability of blocker escalation, scope confusion, or PM rescue loop.
required_feature_groups: communication lag, blocker frequency, scope ambiguity, schedule changes, customer/site volatility
notes: Useful to distinguish execution skill from scope chaos.

purpose: Predict expected duration and overrun vs plan for a proposed job or step family.
required_feature_groups: planned step durations, site/BOM size, complexity, historical cycle times, travel/access conditions
ui_surfaces: Past Jobs, Risk & Conditions, staffing planner
notes: Quantiles are more useful than a single point estimate.

purpose: Predict complaint or poor CSAT risk from behavior/context mismatch.
required_feature_groups: professionalism history, communication style, documentation quality, customer sensitivity, regulated context
notes: Separate from skill; more behavior/customer-fit oriented.

purpose: Predict margin leakage from overruns, extra travel, repeat visits, or admin defects.
required_feature_groups: duration variance, extra trips, expense/timecard issues, parts returns, billing complexity
notes: Useful for preferred-tech logic when economics matter.

purpose: Encode cross-domain technician behavior into universal U_* channels.
required_datasets: fact_dispatch_event; fact_travel_event; fact_arrival_event; fact_tool_check_event; fact_qc_event; fact_artifact_event; fact_escalation_event; fact_closeout_event; fact_feedback_event
required_feature_groups: attendance, travel, arrival, readiness, safety, communication, documentation, closeout, customer experience
label_or_target: show_up, arrival, docs, csat, productivity, safety outcomes
ui_surfaces: Trust decomposition, cross-domain fit, overall reliability
notes: One shared state bundle across all product types.

purpose: Encode product-type-specific skill evidence into D_* channels.
required_feature_groups: step family success, validation quality, execution timing, documentation, domain tools
label_or_target: first_pass, rework, redispatch, defect, duration outcomes by domain
recommended_algorithm: shared trunk + per-domain expert heads / MoE
ui_surfaces: Best fit, weak fit, skills, domain cards
notes: Primary skill engine; do not let one domain bleed into another.

purpose: Capture narrow specialties inside each domain without inflating unrelated work.
required_datasets: bridge_job_domain_scope; fact_runbook_step_event; fact_domain_tool_log; domain subdomain labels
required_feature_groups: subdomain ids, specialty tools, rare step patterns, specialized validations
notes: Avoids fiber work making someone look great at all cabling tasks.

purpose: Encode structured job requirements from YAML/CRM/SOW fields.
required_feature_groups: customer/site/schedule/commercials, service activation, access, BOM-like fields, policies
notes: Context only; must never mutate technician state.

purpose: Encode unstructured notes, SOW text, transcripts, and emails.
required_datasets: fact_job_context_field + source text refs; transcripts; attachments
required_feature_groups: scope text, escalations, nuanced customer/site notes, acceptance criteria
label_or_target: pair fit, issue risk, scope matching
notes: Preserve source evidence spans for explainability.

purpose: Encode repeated site rows, device lists, room lists, and BOM-like collections.
required_feature_groups: unit counts, row diversity, site complexity, repeated install patterns
notes: Important for rollouts and multi-site work.

purpose: Use relationships among technicians, sites, customers, reviewers, assets, and tools as a side signal.
required_datasets: dim_technician; dim_site; dim_customer; fact_job_outcome; fact_domain_tool_log; fact_qc_event
required_feature_groups: co-occurrence edges, reviewer patterns, site recurrence, customer recurrence, substitute overlap
label_or_target: substitute quality, anomaly detection, neighbor agreement
notes: Use as side signal; never the only ranking logic.

purpose: Score final job-tech compatibility after candidate retrieval.
required_datasets: serving_job_context_embedding_cache; serving_tech_embedding_cache; gold_tech_profile_prior; graph side signals
required_feature_groups: J_* context, U_* state, D_* state, subdomain deltas, profile priors, graph priors
recommended_algorithm: MLP / cross network / attention interaction block
ui_surfaces: Best fit, weak fit, shortlist ranking
notes: Primary final scoring model for dispatch.

purpose: Calibrate probabilities and penalize low-evidence / OOD cases.
required_feature_groups: raw logits, evidence count, novelty, drift, slice behavior
notes: Essential for safe production recommendations.

purpose: Assign top work-style archetype and secondary archetypes for UI and explainability.
required_feature_groups: universal/domain embeddings, behavior clusters, proof density
notes: Purely explanatory but highly useful for human trust.

purpose: Learn best/weak time windows and travel conditions for each technician/domain pairing.
required_datasets: fact_job_outcome; dim_job; dim_site; fact_travel_event; fact_arrival_event; fact_technician_domain_state_daily
required_feature_groups: business hours vs after-hours, weekend/overnight, radius bands, remote travel, access windows
label_or_target: relative outcome lift by condition bucket
notes: Only show weak conditions when evidence exceeds threshold.

purpose: Generate viable top-K candidates quickly before reranking.
required_feature_groups: domain embeddings, universal overlays, geo shard, eligibility mask
notes: Keep separate indexes by domain and time horizon.

purpose: Measure whether similar technicians and similar jobs agree with this tech’s projected fit.
required_feature_groups: nearest neighbors, similar job outcomes, reviewer/site overlap
notes: Good sanity check for thin-evidence recommendations.

purpose: Quantify whether a technician is rapidly improving, stable, or regressing in a domain or step family.
required_feature_groups: 30d vs 90d deltas, new runbook adoption, coaching events, step improvement slope
notes: Great for staffing stretch opportunities without overpromising.
